rules.name: no-what-comments-edit
rules.evidence: **NEVER** comment the WHAT of code (the code itself shows what it does)

rules.name: no-what-comments-write
rules.evidence: **NEVER** comment the WHAT of code (the code itself shows what it does)

rules.name: verify-tx-before-signing
rules.evidence: Always verify transaction details before signing

skill_candidates.summary: Local development environment setup: run setup_local.sh, start the node, bootstrap liquidity pools, then set up incentive streams, in that order.
skill_candidates.evidence:
# Setup a local node with default configuration
./scripts/setup_local.sh

# Start local node
dymd start

# Bootstrap liquidity pools
sh scripts/pools/pools_bootstrap.sh

# Setup incentives streams
sh scripts/incentives/fund_incentives.sh

skill_candidates.summary: Offline transaction signing workflow: generate an unsigned tx with --generate-only, sign it with dymd tx sign, then broadcast it with dymd tx broadcast.
skill_candidates.evidence:
# Sign transaction offline
dymd tx bank send wallet1 dym1recipient... 1000000adym \
  --generate-only > unsigned.json
dymd tx sign unsigned.json --from wallet1 > signed.json

# Broadcast signed transaction
dymd tx broadcast signed.json

always_on: Cosmos SDK code all runs in a single thread, there is no multithreading
Transactions rollback if there is any error — atomic, all or nothing; if they panic, nothing happens
BeginBlocker and EndBlocker functions in a module should never panic — it would cause a chain halt
All cosmos validators must get the same result, so the code should be deterministic

Go version 1.23.0 (toolchain 1.24.2); repo uses custom forks of cosmos-sdk, cometbft, ibc-go, and osmosis; proto generation requires Docker; default local chain ID is dymension_100-1; default denom is adym

When verifying code locally, run tools only as needed to avoid unnecessary work (lint/format only if Go changed, proto commands only if .proto changed, tests only for modified packages, full go-acc suite only before final push or if requested)
Never share private keys or mnemonics
Use hardware wallets for mainnet operations
Set appropriate gas limits to avoid overpaying
